source: Composite process lens based on public O'Neil, Minervini, Livermore, and Paul Tudor Jones material. All card language below is paraphrase pending primary-source verification.
_note: Runtime composite lens. It represents trend following with mechanical risk control, not one person's complete philosophy.

master_intro.one_line: Buy demonstrated strength, define risk before entry, cut losses mechanically, and add only to winners.
pillars: Keep losses small and let winners run
master_intro.why_it_matters: This lens treats arguing with a broken trend as the central process failure.

strength_intro: One thing you did well through this lens:

position_sizing.rule: Calculate size from the entry-to-stop distance so one failed trade cannot exceed the risk budget.
position_sizing.quote: Position size follows the loss allowed at the stop. (paraphrase)
position_sizing.motive_q: {max_ticker} is {max_pct}% of the portfolio. What predefined stop and account-risk limit produced that size?

averaging_down.rule: Never add below cost to repair a losing trade; add only after price confirms the thesis and total risk remains capped.
averaging_down.quote: Add to demonstrated winners, not to positions that are proving you wrong. (paraphrase)
averaging_down.motive_q: You added to {tickers} while it was losing. Why was capital added before price reconfirmed the setup?

exit_discipline.rule: Write the stop before entry and execute it without moving it farther away after the loss begins.
exit_discipline.quote: Small losses are a business expense; uncontrolled losses are a process failure. (paraphrase)
exit_discipline.motive_q: When the setup failed, did you execute the original stop or rewrite it to avoid taking the loss?

diversification.rubric_unit: Concentrate in the strongest valid setups
diversification.rule: Hold only the strongest setups whose risks are independently capped; do not add weak names for cosmetic diversification.
diversification.quote: A few strong setups are preferable to many mediocre ones. (paraphrase)
diversification.motive_q: You hold {n} instruments and {ai_pct}% shares one driver. Are all positions among the strongest valid setups, or are weaker names diluting focus?

holding_period.rubric_unit: Hold while the trend remains valid
holding_period.rule: Let winners run while price and trend rules remain valid; exit when the predefined trend condition breaks.
holding_period.quote: The trend, not a calendar label, determines the holding period. (paraphrase)
holding_period.motive_q: For {incon_tickers}, did the trend rule change, or did you relabel the trade after it stopped working?

alpha_beta.rubric_unit: Separate execution edge from market beta
alpha_beta.rule: Decompose market regime and high-beta exposure before crediting entry and exit discipline.
alpha_beta.quote: A strong tape can make poor execution look skilled. (paraphrase)
alpha_beta.motive_q: You beat the benchmark by {excess}pp with beta {beta}. What return came from disciplined execution rather than the market regime?

entry_style.rule: Enter only after price confirms the setup, with a predefined stop and no need to predict the bottom.
entry_style.quote: Let price prove the setup before committing capital. (paraphrase)
entry_style.motive_q: For {entry_ticker}, what price confirmation and stop defined the entry rather than fear of missing out?
